audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for silver cup test, pursuit, Benjamin's sack, brothers' return, Judah's intercession, surety, and substitution offer.

verses.restored: Joseph commanded the one over his house, Fill the men’s sacks with food, as much as they can carry, and put each man’s silver in the mouth of his sack.
Put my cup, the silver cup, in the mouth of the youngest one’s sack, along with his grain silver. He did according to Joseph’s word.
At morning light the men were sent away, they and their donkeys.
They had gone out of the city, not far, when Joseph said to the one over his house, Rise, pursue the men, overtake them, and say, Why have you repaid evil for good?
Is this not the cup from which my lord drinks and by which he surely divines? You have done evil in what you have done.
He overtook them and spoke these words to them.
They said to him, Why does my lord speak such words? Far be it from your servants to do such a thing.
Look, the silver we found in the mouth of our sacks we brought back to you from Canaan. How then would we steal silver or gold from your lord’s house?
With whomever of your servants it is found, let him die, and we also will become servants to my lord.
He said, Now also let it be according to your words: the one with whom it is found shall be my servant, but you shall be innocent.
They quickly lowered each man’s sack to the ground, and each opened his sack.
He searched, beginning with the oldest and ending with the youngest, and the cup was found in Benjamin’s sack.
They tore their garments, loaded each man his donkey, and returned to the city.
Judah and his brothers came to Joseph’s house, and he was still there; they fell before him to the ground.
Joseph said to them, What deed is this that you have done? Did you not know that a man like me surely divines?

Judah said, What shall we say to my lord? What shall we speak, and how shall we justify ourselves? The Divine has found out the guilt of your servants. Look, we are servants to my lord, both we and the one in whose hand the cup was found.
He said, Far be it from me to do this. The man in whose hand the cup was found shall be my servant, but you go up in peace to your father.
Judah came near to him and said, Please, my lord, let your servant speak a word in my lord’s ears, and do not let your anger burn against your servant, for you are like Pharaoh.
My lord asked his servants, saying, Do you have a father or a brother?
We said to my lord, We have an aged father and a young child of his old age. His brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother, and his father loves him.
You said to your servants, Bring him down to me, so I may set my eyes on him.
We said to my lord, The boy cannot leave his father; if he leaves his father, his father will die.
You said to your servants, Unless your youngest brother comes down with you, you shall not see my face again.
When we went up to your servant my father, we told him the words of my lord.
Our father said, Return, buy us a little food.
We said, We cannot go down. If our youngest brother is with us, then we will go down; for we cannot see the man’s face unless our youngest brother is with us.
Your servant my father said to us, You know that my woman bore me two sons.
One went out from me, and I said, Surely he is torn apart, and I have not seen him until now.
If you take this one also from before my face and harm meets him, you will bring down my gray hair in evil to Sheol.
Now, when I come to your servant my father and the boy is not with us, since his soul is bound to his soul,
when he sees that the boy is not there, he will die; and your servants will bring down the gray hair of your servant our father in sorrow to Sheol.
For your servant became surety for the boy to my father, saying, If I do not bring him to you, I will bear the blame before my father all the days.
Now please let your servant remain instead of the boy as a servant to my lord, and let the boy go up with his brothers.
For how can I go up to my father if the boy is not with me? I cannot see the evil that would find my father.
